items.headline: Los Gatos to hold municipal election on November 3, 2026
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for three seats on Tuesday, November 3, 2026. This sets the date for residents to elect council members.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard residential development
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution approving the final tract map for Tract 10675 Boulevard. This clears the way for the subdivision to proceed.

items.headline: Milpitas establishes transit-oriented housing development rules
items.summary: The City Council adopted regulations allowing housing development as a permitted use at transit-oriented development stops, with Tier 1 and Tier 2 stops identified within Milpitas. This streamlines approval for residential projects near transit hubs, removing zoning barriers that previously required case-by-case review.

items.headline: City Council confirms 2026 weed abatement assessment
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution confirming the 2026 Weed Abatement Assessment Report, which identifies properties requiring vegetation removal and establishes costs for abatement work performed by or ordered by the city.

items.headline: Ralph Lauren tenant improvement permitted at Stevens Creek Boulevard
items.summary: A $1.6 million buildout for Ralph Lauren receives a building permit at 2855 Stevens Creek Boulevard. The high-end retailer is expanding its presence in San José's commercial corridor.

items.headline: FY 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant funding discussed
items.summary: The City Council reviewed allocation of federal Community Development Block Grant funds for fiscal year 2026-27. This federal funding supports community development and affordable housing programs in Cupertino.

items.headline: Reformation fashion store permitted at Santana Row
items.summary: Reformation, a sustainable fashion brand, is cleared to build a $464,515 tenant improvement at 333 Santana Row. The retailer's arrival adds another contemporary clothing option to the upscale shopping district.

items.headline: Homeowner seeks variance to reduce rear yard setback for addition
items.summary: A single-family homeowner on an unspecified Sunnyvale street is requesting permission to build a 730-square-foot first-story addition with a rear yard setback of 7 feet 4 inches, below the required 10-foot minimum. The Planning Commission will review design and variance requests on May 27.
